posts.headline: AMrym warns Sudani against splintering from Coordination Framework, calls move a Shiite-on-Shiite conflict project
posts.summary: Direct message to PM Mohammed Shia al-Sudani warning that breaking from the Coordination Framework to form a new alliance would become a project of Shiite-on-Shiite conflict, with militia non-compliance and regional interference inevitable. Closes that power is temporary but history is unforgiving. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: AMrym reads Brent at 107.63 dollars and rising as oil traders pricing in continued escalation
posts.summary: Brief market read framing the 107.63 dollar print and continued upward pressure as a sign that crude traders are not naive on the regional risk premium. Implies the price action is telling a different story than the official ceasefire-recovery narrative. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Knights flags re-enactor research on hoplite warfare crowd dynamics
posts.summary: Quote-tweet on a French research project that trained 200 re-enactors to test hoplite battle and routing dynamics, with Knights reading the footage as a way to make the often vague descriptions in ancient sources newly legible. Off-portfolio for the brief but a tell on Knights' wider frame on conflict mechanics.

posts.headline: Knights on returning resonance of Tanker War imagery as Asia goes short of crude
posts.summary: Quote-tweet on Javier Blas's observation that Japanese television is now treating the arrival of a single American oil tanker as breaking news, framing the moment as a return of high-intensity-war energy logistics imagery and noting that BBC World Service is now leading on a different war every night.

posts.headline: KurdistanWatch on KDP-PUK media war reaching a new low over Trump-supplied weapons claims
posts.summary: Long thread arguing that KDP and PUK media outlets are using state and oil-derived public wealth to fund factional disinformation, with both sides circulating unverified claims about weapons allegedly supplied by Trump and trading fabricated theft accusations. Frames the dispute as a degraded politics where leaders prioritise factional damage over actual regional crises.
